Level_I.requirement: Board approved Cyber Security Policy in place — distinct from IT/IS policy, covering cyber risk approach, threat handling, acceptable risk levels, emerging threats
Level_I.evidence: Board resolution, Approved Cyber Security Policy document

Level_I.requirement: Cyber Security Policy communicated to RBI Regional Office (Dept. of Cooperative Bank Supervision — DCBS)
Level_I.evidence: Submission letter, Acknowledgement from DCBS Regional Office

Level_I.requirement: Cyber Security Policy reviewed annually by Board — updated for new threat landscape, incidents, regulatory changes
Level_I.evidence: Board meeting minutes with policy review, Updated policy versions with change log

Level_I.requirement: Roles and responsibilities clearly defined for cyber security — IT security responsibilities assigned
Level_I.evidence: Organization chart with IT/IS roles, RACI matrix, Job descriptions

Level_I.requirement: Cyber security matters discussed and reviewed in Board meetings — cyber security posture on agenda
Level_I.evidence: Board meeting minutes with cyber security agenda items and discussions

Level_I.requirement: Cyber Crisis Management Plan (CCMP) in place — based on CERT-In/RBI guidelines, integrated with BCP/DR
Level_I.evidence: CCMP document, BCP/DR integration evidence, CCMP test reports

Level_I.requirement: Incident reporting procedure to RBI (DCBS) established — ALL unusual cyber incidents reported without threshold
Level_I.evidence: Incident reporting SOP, Sample past incident reports if any, Escalation matrix

Level_I.requirement: Compliance with CERT-In / NCIIPC / IDRBT guidelines — reference and implement applicable guidelines
Level_I.evidence: Compliance mapping document, CERT-In reporting evidence, NTP configuration, Log retention policy
Level_I.technology: SIEM for log retention, NTP servers

Level_I.requirement: Bank-specific email domain implemented (e.g., bankname.in or bankname.co.in) — no shared/free domain for official communications
Level_I.evidence: Domain registration certificate, Email configuration evidence, Staff communication on official email usage
Level_I.technology: Email server / Microsoft 365 / Google Workspace Business

Level_I.requirement: DMARC controls enforced on email domain — policy at 'reject' or 'quarantine'. SPF record with -all. DKIM configured and signing enabled.
Level_I.evidence: DNS TXT records (DMARC/SPF/DKIM), DMARC aggregate reports, DMARC monitoring dashboard

Level_I.requirement: Anti-phishing controls on email solution — email gateway with phishing detection, suspicious URL blocking, header analysis
Level_I.evidence: Email security solution config, Anti-phishing test results, Blocked phishing attempt logs
Level_I.technology: Email security gateway (Proofpoint/Mimecast/MS Defender for O365)

Level_I.requirement: Anti-malware controls on email solution — attachment scanning, malicious file blocking, sandboxing for suspicious attachments
Level_I.evidence: Email security config showing attachment scanning, Blocked malware reports

Level_I.requirement: Email encryption for sensitive communications — TLS for email transit, encryption for sensitive data attachments
Level_I.evidence: Email server TLS configuration, TLS connection logs, Encryption policy document

Level_I.requirement: Email retention and archival policy — defined retention periods, secure archival, access controls on archived emails
Level_I.evidence: Email retention policy, Archive system configuration, Access logs to archived emails

Level_I.requirement: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for ALL CBS access — 2nd factor MUST be dynamic (OTP/Token). Static password as 2nd factor is NOT acceptable.
Level_I.evidence: 2FA configuration screenshot for CBS, OTP system evidence, Token/OTP logs, User 2FA enrollment records

Level_I.requirement: 2FA for ALL applications connecting to CBS — any application integrating with or accessing CBS must enforce 2FA
Level_I.evidence: Application integration documentation, 2FA configuration for each CBS-connected application

Level_I.requirement: Role-based access control (RBAC) implemented — access based on job function and need-to-know principle
Level_I.evidence: User access matrix, Role definitions per job function, RBAC configuration in CBS and applications

Level_I.requirement: Password policy enforced — complexity (min 8 chars, mixed case, numbers, special), expiry (90 days), history (last 12), lockout (5 failed attempts)
Level_I.evidence: Password policy document, System configuration screenshot (AD/CBS), Policy enforcement evidence
Level_I.technology: Active Directory, CBS password policy module

Level_I.requirement: Privileged access management — admin accounts controlled, all privileged activities logged, periodic review of privileged accounts
Level_I.evidence: Privileged account register, Admin activity logs, Quarterly review records
Level_I.technology: PAM solution (basic) or manual log review

Level_I.requirement: User access review conducted periodically — quarterly or half-yearly review of user access rights for all systems
Level_I.evidence: Access review reports, Manager sign-off records, Remediation evidence for revoked access

Level_I.requirement: User provisioning / de-provisioning process — timely creation on joining, immediate revocation on exit/transfer
Level_I.evidence: User lifecycle SOP, Onboarding checklist, Exit checklist with IT sign-off, Access revocation logs

Level_I.requirement: IT asset inventory maintained — complete register of ALL hardware, software, network devices, key personnel, IT services
Level_I.evidence: IT asset register (Excel/tool), Network discovery scan results, Software license inventory
Level_I.technology: Asset management tool (ManageEngine/Lansweeper) or Excel-based register

Level_I.requirement: Systems storing customer data identified — mapping of all systems/databases that store or process customer data
Level_I.evidence: Data flow diagram, Customer data inventory, System classification list
Level_I.technology: Data mapping tool or DFD software

Level_I.requirement: Asset criticality rating assigned — High/Medium/Low based on data sensitivity and business criticality
Level_I.evidence: Asset classification matrix with criticality ratings, Business impact criteria

Level_I.requirement: Software license management — valid licenses for all software, no pirated or unlicensed software
Level_I.evidence: Software license inventory, License certificates/purchase records, License compliance audit report

Level_I.requirement: Asset disposal / decommissioning process — secure data wiping before disposal, destruction certificates for sensitive assets
Level_I.evidence: Asset disposal SOP, Data wiping logs, Destruction certificates

Level_I.requirement: Firewall deployed and configured — perimeter firewall with documented and reviewed rule sets
Level_I.evidence: Firewall model and configuration, Firewall rule documentation, Quarterly rule review records

Level_I.requirement: Network segregation implemented — CBS network, internet network, ATM network, management network separated using VLANs
Level_I.evidence: Network architecture diagram, VLAN configuration, Firewall rules between segments
Level_I.technology: Managed switches with VLAN support, Firewall

Level_I.requirement: Secure configuration of network devices — default passwords changed, unnecessary services disabled, latest firmware applied
Level_I.frequency: Implement and maintain; firmware updates as released
Level_I.evidence: Network device hardening checklist, Configuration backup, Firmware version records

Level_I.requirement: Wireless network security controls — WPA2/WPA3 encryption, MAC filtering, guest network separated from internal network
Level_I.evidence: Wireless access point configuration, Guest network isolation evidence
Level_I.technology: Enterprise WiFi controller (Cisco/Aruba/Ruckus) or consumer WAP with WPA3

Level_I.requirement: Remote access controls — VPN for ALL remote access. Remote sessions logged. MFA for VPN access.
Level_I.evidence: VPN configuration, MFA setup for VPN, Remote access session logs

Level_I.requirement: Internet access controls — URL filtering, restricted internet access from CBS systems, content filtering
Level_I.evidence: Proxy/filter configuration, URL filtering policy, Internet access policy

Level_I.requirement: Licensed antivirus/anti-malware on ALL systems — automatic updates, scheduled scans, centralized management
Level_I.evidence: AV deployment report, Update status report, Centralized management console screenshot

Level_I.requirement: Operating system patches applied timely — critical patches within 7 days, high within 30 days, medium within 90 days
Level_I.evidence: Patch status report, WSUS/patch management logs, Patching policy document

Level_I.requirement: Application patches applied — third-party applications (Java, Adobe, browsers, MS Office) kept updated
Level_I.evidence: Application inventory with patch status, Third-party update records

Level_I.requirement: USB and removable media controls — USB ports disabled/controlled on CBS and sensitive systems. Data copy to removable media restricted.
Level_I.evidence: USB control policy, GPO/endpoint protection configuration, USB device logs
Level_I.technology: Endpoint security (USB control feature) / Group Policy

Level_I.requirement: Screen lock and session timeout — auto screen lock after maximum 5 minutes inactivity on CBS systems, 10 minutes on other systems
Level_I.evidence: GPO configuration for screen lock, System setting screenshots

Level_I.requirement: Cyber incident response procedure — documented process for detection, containment, eradication, recovery, post-incident review
Level_I.evidence: Incident Response SOP, Contact list with escalation, IR test/tabletop exercise records

Level_I.requirement: Incident logging and tracking — ALL cyber incidents (including minor ones) recorded with details, timelines, actions taken
Level_I.evidence: Incident log/register, Incident tickets

Level_I.requirement: Incident escalation matrix — defined escalation levels, contacts, and timelines for different incident severities
Level_I.evidence: Escalation matrix document, Contact list with phone numbers, Escalation test evidence

Level_I.requirement: Incident reporting to RBI (DCBS) within prescribed timeframe — ALL unusual incidents reported using RBI format (Annex III)
Level_I.evidence: RBI incident reporting template (Annex III), Evidence of past reports sent to DCBS and CERT-In
Level_I.technology: Secure email to DCBS; CERT-In portal (incidents.cert-in.org.in)

Level_I.requirement: Root cause analysis (RCA) for ALL cyber incidents — investigate and document root cause, contributing factors, and corrective actions
Level_I.evidence: RCA reports for each incident, Corrective action records, Verification that corrective actions implemented

Level_I.requirement: Post-incident review and improvement — review response effectiveness, update procedures, share lessons learned
Level_I.frequency: Post each significant incident; annual review of overall IR process
Level_I.evidence: Post-incident review reports, Updated SOPs, Lesson-learned communication records

Level_I.requirement: Cyber security awareness for ALL employees — mandatory annual training, new recruit induction training, regular awareness communications
Level_I.evidence: Training records, Attendance sheets, E-learning completion certificates, Phishing test results

Level_I.requirement: Customer awareness on cyber threats — educate customers on phishing, OTP fraud, vishing, UPI fraud, fake banking apps
Level_I.evidence: Customer awareness materials, SMS campaign records, Website awareness section, Poster images

Level_I.requirement: Vendor / third-party awareness — security expectations communicated to all IT vendors and service providers
Level_I.evidence: Vendor NDA copies, Vendor security briefing records, Contract security clauses

Level_I.requirement: Security training for IT staff — technical training for IT/IS personnel, certification support (CEH, CISSP, etc.)
Level_I.evidence: IT staff training certificates, Attendance records for technical workshops

Level_I.requirement: Vendor security assessment before engagement — due diligence on IT security posture, financial stability, compliance status of all IT vendors
Level_I.evidence: Vendor security questionnaire, Assessment reports, Certification copies
Level_I.technology: Vendor assessment tool or standard questionnaire

Level_I.requirement: Security clauses in ALL vendor contracts — confidentiality, security requirements, right to audit, data handling, incident reporting by vendor
Level_I.evidence: Contract templates with security clauses, Executed agreements

Level_I.requirement: Vendor access controls — limited access, monitoring of vendor remote access, time-limited sessions, no persistent access
Level_I.evidence: Vendor access list, Remote access session logs, Vendor access revocation records
Level_I.technology: VPN with per-user access control, Privileged access management

Level_I.requirement: Data protection with third parties — customer data confidentiality maintained with outsourced vendors, NDA mandatory
Level_I.evidence: NDA copies, Data processing agreements, Vendor data deletion certificates

Level_I.requirement: Grievance redressal mechanism in vendor SLAs — customer complaint resolution process with IT vendors, escalation path
Level_I.evidence: Vendor SLA with grievance redressal clause, Complaint escalation records

Level_I.requirement: Customer data confidentiality controls — protect CIA (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability) of customer data at rest and in transit
Level_I.evidence: Data classification policy, Encryption configuration (CBS/database), TLS configuration
Level_I.technology: Encryption solution, TLS certificate, Database encryption

Level_I.requirement: Database security controls — access controls on databases, audit trails for all DB queries, no direct customer DB access from internet
Level_I.evidence: Database access control list, DB audit logs, Network diagram showing DB isolation
Level_I.technology: Database activity monitoring (basic), DB audit feature

Level_I.requirement: Backup and recovery procedures — regular backups, off-site backup copy, tested restoration at least quarterly
Level_I.evidence: Backup policy, Backup logs, Off-site backup evidence, Quarterly restore test reports

Level_I.requirement: Data retention and disposal policy — defined retention periods per data type, secure disposal at end of retention period
Level_I.evidence: Data retention policy, Disposal records and certificates

Level_I.requirement: Audit trail and logging — logging of ALL critical activities (logins, transactions, config changes, admin actions), log protection, log retention minimum 1 year (180 days per CERT-In)
Level_I.evidence: Log retention policy, Log samples, Log protection configuration (immutable/read-only logs)
Level_I.technology: SIEM or centralized log server (Graylog/ELK/Windows Event Log)

Level_I.requirement: GAICA Report (Gap Assessment & Internal Controls Adequacy) — mandatory before offering digital banking. Submitted via PRAVAAH portal within 30 days of RBI approval. Audited by CERT-In empanelled auditor.
Level_I.reference: UCB Digital Banking Channels Authorisation Directions 2025 (Jan 1, 2026)
Level_I.evidence: GAICA report, CERT-In empanelled auditor credentials, PRAVAAH submission evidence

Level_I.requirement: IPv6-enabled network infrastructure — mandatory for even VIEW-ONLY digital banking. CBS must be operational and stable.
Level_I.reference: UCB Digital Banking Channels Authorisation Directions 2025
Level_I.evidence: IPv6 configuration evidence, Network scan showing IPv6 support, CBS operational status certificate

Level_II.requirement: Appointment of CISO or equivalent senior IT Security Officer — dedicated role, not combined with Head of IT
Level_II.evidence: CISO appointment letter, Job description, Organizational chart showing reporting line

Level_II.requirement: CISO with direct reporting to Board / MD / CEO — independent of IT function, with authority to enforce security policies
Level_II.evidence: Organizational chart, CISO reporting authority documentation

Level_II.requirement: IT Security Committee constituted — cross-functional committee (IT, Risk, Operations, Business) for cyber security oversight
Level_II.evidence: Committee charter, Meeting minutes, Member list and roles
Level_II.technology: Meeting management tool

Level_II.requirement: Dedicated cyber security budget allocated — separate budget line for cyber security initiatives, tools, and training
Level_II.evidence: Budget allocation records, Board approval for cyber security budget

Level_II.requirement: Cyber security KPIs defined and monitored — metrics for security posture reviewed by IT Security Committee and Board
Level_II.evidence: KPI dashboard, IT Security Committee review records, Board reports
Level_II.technology: GRC platform or Excel dashboard

Level_II.requirement: Annual VAPT by CERT-In empanelled auditor — covering all internet-facing systems (internet banking, mobile banking, APIs, payment interfaces)
Level_II.evidence: VAPT report, CERT-In empanelled auditor credentials, Audit engagement letter
Level_II.technology: VAPT tools (Nessus/BurpSuite/Metasploit) used by auditor

Level_II.requirement: VAPT scope covers 100% of internet-facing and critical systems — internet banking, mobile banking, APIs, payment gateways
Level_II.evidence: VAPT scope document, System inventory covered in VAPT, Management approval of scope

Level_II.requirement: VAPT findings remediated within defined timelines — Critical <30 days, High <60 days, Medium <90 days, Low <180 days
Level_II.evidence: VAPT remediation tracker, Remediation evidence, Re-test reports confirming closure
Level_II.technology: Issue tracking tool (Jira/ServiceNow)

Level_II.requirement: Re-testing after significant changes — VAPT must be conducted after any major change to internet-facing systems or infrastructure
Level_II.evidence: Change management records, Re-test reports after changes, Change approval including VAPT requirement

Level_II.requirement: VAPT results reviewed by CISO and IT Security Committee — management sign-off on VAPT findings and remediation plan
Level_II.evidence: IT Security Committee meeting minutes discussing VAPT, Management sign-off records, Board VAPT summary

Level_II.requirement: Secure HTTPS with valid EV SSL/TLS certificate — TLS 1.2 minimum (TLS 1.3 preferred), HSTS enabled, security headers configured
Level_II.evidence: SSL certificate details, TLS configuration report (ssllabs.com grade A minimum), Security headers test evidence
Level_II.technology: SSL certificate, TLS management, WAF for security headers

Level_II.requirement: MFA for ALL internet banking transactions — dynamic OTP mandatory for login and each transaction above threshold amount
Level_II.evidence: Internet banking MFA configuration, OTP system evidence, Transaction OTP logs
Level_II.technology: OTP platform (SMS/TOTP), Internet banking system with MFA

Level_II.requirement: Real-time transaction monitoring for internet banking — detect and alert on unusual/suspicious transaction patterns
Level_II.evidence: Transaction monitoring system config, Alert rules, Blocked transaction records
Level_II.technology: Transaction monitoring system or FRM system

Level_II.requirement: Session management controls — session timeout (≤15 mins idle), concurrent session limits, secure cookie attributes (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite)
Level_II.evidence: Internet banking session configuration, Penetration test evidence of session controls
Level_II.technology: WAF, Secure coding standards

Level_II.requirement: Secure customer registration and authentication — online KYC verification, account linking security, multi-step registration
Level_II.evidence: Customer registration SOP, KYC verification evidence, Registration security test results
Level_II.technology: Internet banking platform with KYC integration

Level_II.requirement: Transaction limit controls — per-transaction limits, daily limits, customer-configurable limits (but not above RBI prescribed limits)
Level_II.evidence: Transaction limit configuration in CBS/internet banking, RBI limit compliance evidence
Level_II.technology: CBS system, Internet banking platform

Level_II.requirement: Customer notification for ALL transactions — real-time SMS and email alerts for every debit/credit transaction
Level_II.evidence: SMS gateway configuration, Alert trigger evidence, Customer notification policy
Level_II.technology: SMS gateway, Internet banking notification module

Level_II.requirement: Mobile app security assessment — VAPT for mobile app following OWASP MSTG framework, before launch and after major updates
Level_II.evidence: Mobile app VAPT report, OWASP MSTG compliance evidence, Auditor credentials
Level_II.technology: Mobile app security testing tools (MobSF/Frida/Objection)

Level_II.requirement: Device binding for mobile banking — bind app to registered device using combination of hardware, software, and service identifiers
Level_II.evidence: Device binding technical implementation evidence, Registration process documentation
Level_II.technology: Mobile banking SDK with device binding, HSM for device identity

Level_II.requirement: Mobile app published on official stores ONLY — Google Play Store and Apple App Store only. No APK download from website or third-party stores.
Level_II.evidence: Official app store listing, Customer communication about official source, Fake app monitoring reports
Level_II.technology: App store developer account, Fake app detection service

Level_II.requirement: Certificate pinning implemented in mobile app — prevent MITM attacks by pinning server's SSL certificate in the app
Level_II.evidence: Certificate pinning implementation evidence, VAPT test confirming MITM prevention
Level_II.technology: Mobile app development with certificate pinning SDK

Level_II.requirement: Remote wipe / lock / deactivation of mobile banking app — ability to disable compromised app accounts remotely
Level_II.evidence: Remote deactivation process documentation, Test evidence of remote wipe capability
Level_II.technology: Mobile banking platform with device management API

Level_II.requirement: DLP solution implemented — monitor and prevent unauthorized exfiltration of sensitive customer data
Level_II.evidence: DLP solution deployment report, DLP policy configuration, Alert records
Level_II.technology: DLP (Symantec/Forcepoint/McAfee/Microsoft Purview)

Level_II.requirement: DLP policy covers email and web channels — prevent sending/uploading customer data via email or web
Level_II.evidence: DLP policy showing email and web channel coverage, Blocked incident records
Level_II.technology: DLP with email and web gateway integration

Level_II.requirement: DLP alerts reviewed and actioned within defined SLA — security team reviews and investigates all DLP alerts
Level_II.evidence: DLP alert review records, Investigation reports, Action taken log
Level_II.technology: DLP management console, Incident management tool

Level_II.requirement: Privileged Identity Management (PIM) solution — manage, control, monitor, and audit privileged accounts
Level_II.evidence: PIM solution deployment, Privileged session recording evidence, JIT access configuration
Level_II.technology: PAM/PIM tool (CyberArk/BeyondTrust/Thycotic)

Level_II.requirement: Jump server / bastion host for ALL administrative access to servers and critical systems
Level_II.evidence: Jump server configuration, Access logs showing all sessions via jump server
Level_II.technology: Bastion host (JumpCloud/Teleport/custom hardened server)

Level_II.requirement: MFA for VPN and ALL remote access — mandatory MFA for any remote access to bank systems including vendor access
Level_II.evidence: VPN MFA configuration, Connection logs showing MFA enforcement
Level_II.technology: VPN with MFA integration (FortiClient/OpenVPN with TOTP)

Level_II.requirement: Centralized log management (SIEM) — aggregate logs from all critical systems for correlation and analysis
Level_II.evidence: SIEM deployment evidence, Log source inventory, Correlation rule library
Level_II.technology: SIEM (Splunk/QRadar/Elastic/Graylog)

Level_II.requirement: Security event alerting and response — defined alert rules for critical security events with SLA for response
Level_II.evidence: SIEM alert rule configuration, Alert review records, Response SLA documentation
Level_II.technology: SIEM with alerting, SOC workflow tool

Level_II.requirement: 24x7 monitoring of internet banking and mobile banking systems — availability and security monitoring round-the-clock
Level_II.evidence: NOC/SOC arrangement evidence, Monitoring tool config, 24x7 on-call roster
Level_II.technology: Monitoring tool (PRTG/Nagios/Zabbix), Automated alerting

Level_II.requirement: Log retention for minimum 1 year — all security logs retained for minimum 1 year (CERT-In: 180 days with 6-hour retrieval)
Level_II.evidence: Log retention policy, Storage configuration, Sample log retrieval test
Level_II.technology: Log storage (NAS/cloud storage), SIEM retention config

Level_II.requirement: IT vendor risk management framework — structured approach to identifying, assessing, monitoring, and managing IT vendor risks
Level_II.evidence: Vendor risk framework document, Vendor risk register, Ongoing monitoring records
Level_II.technology: Vendor risk management tool (or structured Excel)

Level_II.requirement: Right to audit clause in all vendor contracts — contractual right for bank to conduct or commission security audit of vendor
Level_II.evidence: Contract clauses showing right to audit, Audit exercise records for critical vendors

Level_II.requirement: Exit management plan for critical vendors — documented plan for vendor transition or exit without disruption to bank operations
Level_II.evidence: Exit management plan for each critical vendor, Alternative vendor list, Data retrieval SOP

Level_III.requirement: ATM network security — dedicated VLAN for ALL ATM infrastructure, isolated from CBS and internet networks
Level_III.reference: Annex III 2019 + ATM Switch Circular Dec 2019
Level_III.evidence: ATM network diagram, VLAN configuration showing ATM isolation, Firewall rules between ATM and CBS networks
Level_III.technology: Managed switches with ATM VLAN, NGFW

Level_III.requirement: ATM switch application access controls — strict access control on ATM switch systems, dual authorization for critical functions
Level_III.evidence: ATM switch access control list, Dual authorization evidence, Access audit logs
Level_III.technology: PAM tool, ATM switch management system

Level_III.requirement: Physical security for ATM switch infrastructure — data centre hosting, biometric access, CCTV surveillance
Level_III.evidence: Physical access logs for ATM switch room, CCTV records, DC physical security audit
Level_III.technology: Biometric access system, CCTV, DC physical security controls

Level_III.requirement: ATM transaction monitoring for fraud — real-time monitoring of all ATM transactions, velocity checks, geo-anomaly detection
Level_III.evidence: ATM fraud monitoring system configuration, Alert records, Blocked transaction evidence
Level_III.technology: ATM fraud management system, Transaction monitoring tool

Level_III.requirement: ATM switch DR and failover capability — disaster recovery site for ATM switch operations with tested failover
Level_III.evidence: DR site for ATM switch, Failover test reports, RTO/RPO evidence for ATM switch
Level_III.technology: ATM switch DR setup, Replication technology

Level_III.requirement: PCI-DSS compliance for card operations — maintain PCI-DSS compliance for all card data storage, processing, and transmission
Level_III.evidence: PCI-DSS compliance certificate (SAQ D or ROC), QSA assessment report, Tokenization implementation evidence
Level_III.technology: PCI-compliant HSM, Tokenization platform, EMV infrastructure

Level_III.requirement: SWIFT Customer Security Programme (CSP) compliance — annual attestation via KYC-SA covering all mandatory controls
Level_III.evidence: SWIFT CSP attestation, KYC-SA report, Mandatory control implementation evidence
Level_III.technology: SWIFT software, CSP-compliant security tools

Level_III.requirement: SWIFT network isolation — SWIFT environment completely isolated from all other bank systems and internet
Level_III.evidence: SWIFT network diagram showing isolation, Firewall rules protecting SWIFT environment
Level_III.technology: Dedicated SWIFT network infrastructure, NGFW

Level_III.requirement: SWIFT message monitoring — monitor ALL SWIFT messages for unusual patterns, unauthorized modifications, duplicate messages
Level_III.evidence: SWIFT transaction monitoring configuration, Alert records, Manual review logs
Level_III.technology: SWIFT transaction monitoring (SWIFT Transaction Screening or third-party)

Level_III.requirement: SWIFT operator reconciliation — daily reconciliation of all SWIFT messages sent and received
Level_III.evidence: Daily reconciliation reports, Exception resolution records
Level_III.technology: SWIFT reconciliation module, CBS integration

Level_III.requirement: SWIFT access limited to authorized users with dual control — minimum two-person rule for all SWIFT transactions
Level_III.evidence: SWIFT access matrix, Dual authorization configuration, User role list
Level_III.technology: SWIFT system with dual control, PAM for SWIFT users

Level_III.requirement: Data centre physical security — biometric access, mantraps, CCTV, visitor management, no unauthorized entry
Level_III.evidence: Biometric access logs, CCTV records, Visitor log, Physical security audit report
Level_III.technology: Biometric access system, CCTV, Mantrap infrastructure

Level_III.requirement: Data centre environmental controls — UPS, generator, precision AC, temperature/humidity monitoring, fire suppression
Level_III.evidence: Environmental monitoring reports, UPS/generator test records, Fire suppression maintenance records
Level_III.technology: Environmental monitoring system (DCIM), UPS/generator

Level_III.requirement: DC hosting agreement security clauses — if co-located, security SLAs and audit rights in hosting agreement
Level_III.evidence: Co-location agreement with security SLAs, Audit rights clause, Past audit reports of DC provider

Level_III.requirement: Data residency — ALL customer data hosted within India. No cross-border data transfer without RBI approval.
Level_III.reference: Annex III 2019 + IT Outsourcing MD 2023 + DPDP Rules 2025
Level_III.evidence: DC location certificates, Cloud data residency proof, Data residency policy
Level_III.technology: India-based cloud regions (AWS Mumbai/Azure India/GCP Mumbai)

Level_III.requirement: Capacity planning and performance monitoring for DC infrastructure — regular capacity reviews, performance dashboards
Level_III.evidence: Annual capacity review report, Performance monitoring dashboard
Level_III.technology: DC infrastructure monitoring (Nagios/PRTG/Zabbix)

Level_III.requirement: IDS/IPS on critical network segments — CBS network, payment networks, SWIFT network, internet-facing DMZ
Level_III.evidence: IDS/IPS deployment architecture, Rule version records, SIEM integration evidence, Alert response records
Level_III.technology: IDS/IPS (Snort/Suricata/Cisco IPS/Palo Alto IDS)

Level_III.requirement: Web Application Firewall (WAF) for ALL internet-facing applications — internet banking, mobile banking APIs, web portals
Level_III.evidence: WAF deployment evidence, Rule set configuration, Blocked attack logs, WAF monthly reports
Level_III.technology: WAF (F5 ASM/Imperva/Cloudflare/AWS WAF/ModSecurity)

Level_III.requirement: Anti-DDoS protection for internet-facing services — scrubbing center or cloud-based DDoS mitigation
Level_III.evidence: Anti-DDoS configuration, DDoS mitigation test evidence, Scrubbing service agreement
Level_III.technology: Anti-DDoS (Cloudflare/Akamai/AWS Shield/Radware)

Level_III.requirement: Threat intelligence feeds integrated — consume IOC feeds from CERT-In, IDRBT, IB-CART, commercial TI providers
Level_III.evidence: TI feed subscriptions, SIEM IOC integration evidence, Threat intel reports actioned
Level_III.technology: Threat intel platform (MISP/Anomali) or SIEM TI integration

Level_III.requirement: Advanced malware protection — sandboxing for suspicious files and email attachments, behavior-based detection
Level_III.evidence: Sandbox configuration, Malware analysis reports, EDR deployment evidence
Level_III.technology: Email/web sandbox (Palo Alto WildFire/Proofpoint TAP), EDR (CrowdStrike/SentinelOne)

Level_III.requirement: RBTM (Risk-Based Transaction Monitoring) solution deployed — ML-based or rule-based system for real-time transaction risk scoring
Level_III.evidence: RBTM system deployment evidence, Vendor details, Integration with CBS/internet banking
Level_III.technology: RBTM (FICO Falcon/SAS Fraud/ACI ReD Shield/custom ML system)

Level_III.requirement: RBTM rules tuned for UCB-specific fraud patterns — custom rules for cooperative bank transaction patterns and fraud types
Level_III.evidence: RBTM rule configuration, False positive rate metrics, Quarterly rule review records
Level_III.technology: RBTM system with rule management

Level_III.requirement: RBTM alerts reviewed within defined SLA — critical alerts within 15 minutes, high within 1 hour, medium within 4 hours
Level_III.evidence: RBTM SLA document, Alert review records, Escalation records for SLA breaches
Level_III.technology: RBTM alert management, SOC workflow tool

Level_III.requirement: RBTM integrated with incident management — high-risk RBTM alerts automatically create incident tickets in IR system
Level_III.evidence: Integration configuration between RBTM and incident management, Sample incident records created from RBTM
Level_III.technology: RBTM + Incident management tool with API integration

Level_IV.requirement: C-SOC (Cyber SOC) established with 24x7 monitoring capability — dedicated staffing, tools, processes
Level_IV.evidence: C-SOC setup documentation, Staffing roster, Tool deployment evidence, SOC SOP
Level_IV.technology: SIEM, SOAR, Threat intel platform, SOC management tool

Level_IV.requirement: SOC use-case library defined and maintained — MITRE ATT&CK mapped detection use-cases for UCB-specific threats
Level_IV.evidence: SOC use-case library document, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, Detection rule library in SIEM
Level_IV.technology: SIEM with rule library, MITRE ATT&CK navigator

Level_IV.requirement: SOC SLA and escalation process — defined response SLAs per incident severity, escalation path to CISO and Board
Level_IV.evidence: SOC SLA document, Escalation matrix, SLA compliance reports
Level_IV.technology: SOC workflow tool, Incident management system

Level_IV.requirement: SOC performance metrics and monthly reports to Board — monthly SOC KPI report, quarterly Board SOC summary
Level_IV.frequency: Monthly to CISO; quarterly to Board
Level_IV.evidence: Monthly SOC performance reports, Quarterly Board SOC presentations

Level_IV.requirement: Threat hunting capability in C-SOC — proactive threat hunting exercises to find hidden threats not detected by automated tools
Level_IV.evidence: Threat hunting plan, Hunt reports, IOCs identified, Remediation from hunting
Level_IV.technology: Threat hunting tools (Elastic/Splunk for hunting), Threat intel

Level_IV.requirement: SOC integrated with Incident Response team — SOC alerts directly trigger IR process, SOAR integration
Level_IV.evidence: SOAR integration evidence, Runbook library, SOC-to-IR handoff records

Level_IV.requirement: Board IT/Cyber Security Committee — dedicated Board-level committee for IT and cyber security oversight
Level_IV.evidence: Board IT Committee charter, Quarterly meeting minutes, Member profiles

Level_IV.requirement: IT Strategy document — comprehensive IT strategy reviewed and approved by Board annually, aligned with business strategy
Level_IV.evidence: IT strategy document, Board approval minutes, Annual review evidence

Level_IV.requirement: IT Risk Management Framework — structured approach to IT risk identification, assessment, treatment, monitoring, reporting
Level_IV.evidence: IT risk framework document, IT risk register, Risk treatment plans, Board risk reports

Level_IV.requirement: IT Steering Committee at Senior Management level — cross-functional committee for IT governance, quarterly meetings
Level_IV.evidence: IT Steering Committee charter, Meeting minutes

Level_IV.requirement: Cyber security scorecard/metrics at Board level — quarterly cyber security KPI presentation to Board
Level_IV.evidence: Quarterly Board cyber security presentations, Board meeting minutes with cyber agenda

Level_IV.requirement: IT Audit aligned to IS audit standards — internal IT audit using COBIT/IS audit standards, IS audit plan approved by Audit Committee
Level_IV.evidence: IT audit plan, IS audit reports, Audit Committee meeting minutes, Finding closure records

Level_IV.requirement: Zero Trust Architecture — never trust, always verify for ALL network access. Identity-centric security model.
Level_IV.evidence: Zero Trust design document, Implementation evidence, Access policy based on identity not network zone
Level_IV.technology: Zero Trust platform (Zscaler/Okta/Palo Alto Prisma Access)

Level_IV.requirement: Cyber deception technology (honeypots/honeynets) — deploy deception assets to detect attackers early
Level_IV.evidence: Honeypot deployment evidence, Deception technology config, Alert records from honeypot triggers

Level_IV.requirement: Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) — advanced behavior-based endpoint security replacing or supplementing traditional AV
Level_IV.evidence: EDR deployment report, Agent coverage evidence, Threat detection and response records

Level_IV.requirement: Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) — continuous monitoring of cloud configurations for misconfigurations and compliance
Level_IV.evidence: CSPM deployment, Misconfiguration reports, Remediation records

Level_IV.requirement: API Security Gateway — centralized API gateway with security controls for all external and internal APIs
Level_IV.evidence: API gateway configuration, API inventory, Authentication and rate limit configuration evidence

Level_IV.requirement: Micro-segmentation of critical workloads — network micro-segmentation for CBS, payment systems, SWIFT to limit lateral movement
Level_IV.evidence: Micro-segmentation design, Network policy evidence, East-west traffic control validation

Level_IV.requirement: Annual IS Audit by CERT-In empanelled auditor — comprehensive IS audit covering all domains (IT governance, infrastructure, security, BCP/DR, applications)
Level_IV.evidence: IS audit report (CERT-In empanelled auditor), Audit scope document, Auditor credentials
Level_IV.technology: IS audit tools used by auditor

Level_IV.requirement: IS Audit findings tracked and remediated — formal tracking system with owner, timeline, evidence of remediation
Level_IV.evidence: Audit finding tracker, Remediation evidence per finding, Closure sign-off records

Level_IV.requirement: Concurrent audit mechanism for IT systems — ongoing concurrent audit as part of internal audit function
Level_IV.evidence: Concurrent audit plan, Audit reports, Internal audit team credentials

Level_IV.requirement: RBI compliance self-assessment submitted annually — formal self-assessment against RBI cyber security framework submitted to RBI
Level_IV.evidence: Completed self-assessment report, Management sign-off, RBI submission evidence

Level_IV.requirement: Regulatory compliance calendar — maintain tracker for all RBI, CERT-In, DPDP, RBI circular compliance deadlines
Level_IV.evidence: Compliance calendar document, Deadline tracking evidence

Level_IV.requirement: External penetration testing by Red Team — annual adversarial red team exercise simulating sophisticated attackers
Level_IV.evidence: Red team engagement scope, Red team report, Remediation plan

Level_IV.requirement: Full BCP/DR test annually with actual system switchover — validate RTO/RPO targets with documented test results
Level_IV.evidence: DR test plan, DR test reports with actual RTO/RPO metrics, IT Steering Committee review

Level_IV.requirement: Cyber resilience plan — separate from BCP, specifically for cyber incidents: ransomware recovery, data breach response, DDoS resilience
Level_IV.evidence: Cyber resilience plan document, Ransomware recovery playbook, Isolated recovery environment
Level_IV.technology: Immutable backup solution, Isolated recovery network

Level_IV.requirement: Crisis communication plan — communication protocol for cyber incidents with customers, media, regulators, staff
Level_IV.evidence: Crisis communication plan, Communication templates, Spokesperson designation

Level_IV.requirement: Tabletop cyber crisis exercises for Board and senior management — annual simulation of cyber crisis scenarios
Level_IV.evidence: Tabletop exercise agenda, Scenario details, Participant list, Exercise report, Lessons learned
Level_IV.technology: Exercise simulation tools or facilitated workshop

Level_IV.requirement: RTO < 4 hours for ALL critical systems — CBS, internet banking, payment processing must recover within 4 hours of disaster
Level_IV.evidence: RTO targets document, Annual DR test showing actual RTO for each critical system

Level_IV.requirement: Cyber insurance policy in place — appropriate cyber insurance coverage for data breach, ransomware, business interruption, regulatory fines
Level_IV.evidence: Cyber insurance policy document, Coverage details, Annual renewal evidence
